mission.title: keylime: EK Certificate Verification Fails Due to Issuer Mismatch in Keylime Agent's Retrieved Certificate
mission.description: EK Certificate Verification Fails Due to Issuer Mismatch in Keylime Agent's Retrieved Certificate. This issue affects 0+ users.

steps.title: Identify keylime troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your keylime deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime -n keylime --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `EK signature did not match certificates from TPM cert store`.`

steps.title: Review keylime configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keylime configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime
kubectl get configmap -n keylime -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keylime
```
When attempting to add an agent using keylime_tenant -add, I'm consistently encountering the error:
```
2025-06-27 11:38:11.922 - keylime.cert_utils - WARNING - Failed to parse PEM data with python-cryptography (might not be strictly conforming to

steps.title: Apply the fix for EK Certificate Verification Fails Due to Issuer Mismatch in
steps.description:
# fix: models: Do not re-encode certificate stored in DB

## Type of Change
*(Select all that apply)*

## Related Issues
**Resolves:** #1775 

## Change Description
Modify the Certificate class to store the original certificate bits to the database instead of storing the possibly re-encoded
```yaml
2025-06-27 11:38:11.922 - keylime.cert_utils - WARNING - Failed to parse PEM data with python-cryptography (might not be strictly conforming to DER ASN.1 encoding): error parsing asn1 value: ParseError { kind: InvalidSetOrdering, location: ["Certificate::tbs_cert", "TbsCertificate::issuer", 0, 2] }
2025-06-27 11:38:11.928 - keylime.cert_utils - ERROR - No Root CA matched EK Certificate
2025-06-27 11:38:11.928 - keylime.tenant - WARNING - Invalid EK certificate for Agent d432fbb3-d2f1-4a97-9ef7-75bd81c00000 (130.195.10.183:9002)
2025-06-27 11:38:11.928 - keylime.tenant - ERROR - TPM Quote from 
```

steps.title: Confirm EK Certificate Verification Fails Due to Issuer is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keylime -n keylime --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n keylime --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `EK signature did not match certificates from TPM cert store`.` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
# fix: models: Do not re-encode certificate stored in DB

## Type of Change
*(Select all that apply)*

## Related Issues
**Resolves:** #1775 

## Change Description
Modify the Certificate class to store the original certificate bits to the database instead of storing the possibly re-encoded certificate.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keylime installed or the issue environment reproducible.
